surprise_log.observation: A hurricane warning issued by the National-Hurricane-Center NHC in Miami in coordination with the NWS Tampa-Bay weather-forecast-office and the NWS Key-West weather-forecast-office and the Florida-Division-of-Emergency-Management FDEM posted onto the multi-jurisdictional alert-channel covering the Florida-southwest-Gulf-Coast Sarasota-Charlotte-Lee-Collier-Monroe county-band along the eastern-Gulf-of-Mexico hurricane-corridor on the trailing-hour issuance window ahead of a projected Category-Four major-hurricane landfall window on the Florida-southwest-Gulf-Coast barrier-island corridor within the next thirty-six-hour-and-forty-eight-hour landfall-arrival cone-of-uncertainty: the NHC track-and-intensity advisory cycle surfaces a slow-moving steerable Hurricane Beatrix sub-tropical-Atlantic-warm-pool-energized system with one-hundred-and-thirty-five-mile-per-hour Category-Four sustained-wind intensity on the Saffir-Simpson hurricane-wind-scale plus a nine-hundred-and-thirty-millibar central-pressure pull plus a forty-mile-radius eyewall-radius-of-maximum-winds RMW pattern plus a tightening-eye eyewall-replacement-cycle ERC pass surfacing a small-eyewall-radius high-intensity inner-core picture plus an expanding tropical-storm-force-wind radius spanning a one-hundred-and-eighty-mile band on the windward right-front quadrant. The Category-Four major-hurricane human-impact picture surfaces a roughly one-point-eight-million-resident permanent-population band across the five-county Sarasota-Charlotte-Lee-Collier-Monroe warning-zone with a heavily-concentrated elder-resident share clustered onto the senior-living-and-assisted-living-and-continuing-care-retirement-community CCRC residential-fleet across the Naples-and-Marco-Island and Fort-Myers-and-Cape-Coral and Punta-Gorda and Sarasota-and-Venice and Key-West-and-Marathon barrier-island-and-coastal-mainland residential corridor, with a per-county special-needs medical-needs-population register on the home-medical-equipment HME oxygen-and-dialysis-and-mobility-impaired roster pulling roughly fifty-five-thousand registered residents into the priority-evacuation queue, and a per-county marginally-housed-and-unsheltered population pull pattern that the county-side homelessness-services outreach teams are racing to contact ahead of the wind-and-surge arrival window. The per-county hospital-and-skilled-nursing-facility evacuation-cycle lifts roughly fourteen-thousand acute-care inpatients and roughly twenty-eight-thousand long-term-care residents onto receiving-facility transfer pathways under per-facility evacuation-plan-and-receiving-facility-agreement document-cycles covering critical-power-and-fuel-supply continuity, medical-records-and-medication-supply travel-pack staging, and per-resident chain-of-custody handoff documentation, while the per-county ambulatory-tourism-and-second-home seasonal-resident pull adds an estimated three-hundred-thousand visitor-and-snowbird population layer that the county-side tourism-and-visitor-bureau emergency-messaging cycle is now pushing through hotel-and-vacation-rental-and-AirBnB short-term-rental contact-channels in English-and-Spanish-and-Haitian-Creole language bands. The downstream post-landfall human-recovery horizon surfaces a per-household displaced-resident shelter-intake census projection in the eighty-thousand-to-one-hundred-twenty-thousand-resident band on the per-county American-Red-Cross general-population and special-needs medical-needs-shelter SpNS surfaces, a per-household individual-assistance FEMA-IA application-and-disbursement cycle that historical Category-Four Florida-landfall analogues project to land in the four-hundred-thousand-to-six-hundred-thousand registered-applicant band, and a per-household and per-small-business volunteer-organizations-active-in-disaster VOAD long-term-recovery-group case-management horizon extending eighteen-months-to-three-years post-event on the per-municipality unmet-needs-roundtable casework cadence.
